$comment: DURABLE roadmap spec. It holds INTENT (what each item means) and a PREDICATE (a machine check that proves whether it exists in the repo) — never a hand-written status. `npm run novakai:roadmap` computes built/partial/unverified/missing live from these predicates, so this file cannot go stale the way a prose '**State:** ❌ Missing' line does. Editing an `intent` is a design decision; editing `checks` changes what 'done' means and must match real artifacts.
spine: understand -> (continue | design) -> plan -> review -> approve -> implement -> re-sync

keystones.1: testable understanding — makes the handover trusted (Phase B)
keystones.2: behavioural acceptance tests in the contract — makes implementation error-free, not just correctly-shaped (E2)

checkKinds.grep: { path, pattern } — regex pattern occurs in file (missing file = fail, not crash)
checkKinds.manual: { note } — declared NOT machine-verifiable yet (behavioural/visual). Never auto-passes; surfaces as 'unverified', distinct from 'missing'.

statusRule: auto = checks except manual. passed==total && !manual -> built. passed==total && manual -> partial (pieces proven, behaviour unverified). 0<passed<total -> partial. total>0 && passed==0 -> missing. total==0 && manual -> unverified.

items.title: Symbol-level completeness gate
items.intent: Every exported symbol must be a node or an explicit, listed curation exclusion. Closes the hole that caused the original drift.

items.title: Edge verification
items.intent: Every map edge is code-backed (the source file imports the target, or the endpoints are co-located) OR an audited advisory edge (a deliberate ctx.hooks/runtime edge in edge-advisory-allowlist.txt). Closes the blind spot where the whole call graph the human review trusts for blast-radius was unverified.

items.title: Code<->map freshness in CI
items.intent: CI regenerates the node set + signatures from code and fails on divergence, not just bundle==fragments. Makes main un-stale-able.

items.title: Two-parser conformance test
items.intent: Prove the app parser (io/mermaid) and pipeline parser (mmd-parse) produce identical models, so review and enforcement never disagree.

items.title: Verification-tier metadata
items.intent: Every claim tagged verified / advisory / unverified, with a report emitting PROVEN vs NARRATED so a fresh agent can calibrate trust.

items.title: Single onboarding command (novakai:onboard)
items.intent: One door in: proves the map green, prints the durable invariants, points at the verified artifacts, hands off the quiz. Keystone-1 entry.

items.title: Keystone 1 — comprehension self-test
items.intent: A deterministic question set generated from the verified map; a fresh agent answers from the map alone and is scored against it. Understanding becomes pass/fail.

items.title: Verified work-state (novakai:status)
items.intent: Scenario 1. 'Where we left off' is derived per-change from the live gate (built / pending / drifted / missing), not stored prose. Kills the high-risk continuity break point.

items.title: Plan authoring + one-command dry-run cert
items.intent: Scenario 2. English -> plan.json + proposed .mmd, then one command runs apply -> stubs -> tsc -> gate and certifies the plan round-trips (delta vs base) before a human opens it.

items.title: Authoring-time coherence
items.intent: Plan references only real ids, dependency graph acyclic, accepted set coherent — checked at authoring time, not only in the review UI.

items.title: Layout fidelity
items.intent: The review overlay renders on the human's real ctx.state positions (levelPositions), not a synthesised force-sim layout. The canvas is the human layer.

items.title: Unified review surface
items.intent: diffWorkspace and planner collapse to one path: the planner is the single review surface, reviewing an authored plan.json OR a raw proposal .mmd (planFromDiff derives a plan from the before/after diff). Both toolbar buttons route into it.

items.title: Single approval export
items.intent: One artifact: approved .mmd + generated stubs/contracts + the gate flipped to 'unbuilt' = the build checklist.

items.title: Keystone 2 — behavioural acceptance tests in the contract
items.intent: An approved change carries acceptance cases run against the REAL implemented symbol (resolved via the map's %% src) — red until implemented, green after. 'done' = signature-gate green AND these behavioural cases green.

items.title: Writeback
items.intent: Approved/implemented code updates the fragments automatically: scaffold --add-from-plan appends an approved plan's new nodes into a fragment, so the loop closes without the manual fragment edit that caused the drift.

items.title: CI enforces the whole loop
items.intent: A merge can't land unless the map is fresh+complete (A1/A2), the in-flight plan still certifies (C2), the behavioural acceptance mechanism passes (E2), and writeback + layout fidelity hold (E3/D1). 'Error-free' becomes a CI property.

items.title: Session protocol in CLAUDE.md
items.intent: The agent working-protocol (onboard -> quiz -> build with subagents -> 0-context verify -> re-sync + handoff) is durably recorded as intent in CLAUDE.md, so a fresh chat inherits it instead of needing it re-explained. The meta-loop's prose half.

items.title: SessionStart onboard hook
items.intent: A harness hook runs novakai:onboard at session start, so every fresh/resumed chat opens with the proven map + quiz prompt as context — onboarding is forced, not remembered.

items.title: Stop handoff-freshness hook
items.intent: A Stop hook runs handoff-fresh.mjs as a nudge when a session ends with the handoff lagging the code, so the continuity artifact is kept current. The nudge half; F4 is the gate half.

items.title: Verifiable meta-loop predicate
items.intent: The meta-loop is enforced by TRUTH, not timestamps: the handoff must make no claim the committed tree falsifies (handoff-fresh --check exits non-zero when a handoff claim is provably false, e.g. 'not yet committed' about a committed file). CI blocks a merge whose handoff lies about the code. Timestamp staleness is a non-blocking Stop-hook nudge to re-sync each session, not a per-PR merge blocker (that coupled every code PR to a bump of one shared file and dead-locked on parallel PRs).

items.title: End-to-end loop run
items.intent: The whole spine (plan-check -> cert -> approve-export -> status -> writeback -> edges) runs as ONE chained sequence on the real in-flight plan, proving the loop executes end-to-end, not just that each link passes in isolation.

items.title: Per-change contract packet emitter (novakai:contract)
items.intent: A 0-context subagent receives a self-contained, byte-deterministic packet per change (target, source path#symbol, proposed signature, behavioural acceptance cases, real downstream blast radius, deps, coherence) and nothing it must interpret from prose. Routes to existing computation (parseMmd, srcDirectives, the REAL downstreamCone, checkPlan); never recomputes. Carries a content hash.

items.title: Closed-form per-change verdict (novakai:verify-change)
items.intent: The result that escapes a subagent is a tool-computed, data-only verdict (structural status enum + behavioural pass counts + content hash), not a model-judged narration. Routes to status.mjs --json + runAcceptance and folds them into PASS/FAIL. 100 correct solutions return 100 byte-identical verdicts.

items.title: Determinism harness / 100->100 proof (novakai:replay)
items.intent: The binding proof of the whole idea: a task run N times must yield ONE stdout hash and ONE exit status, or it is computing/judging instead of routing. The predicate runs the harness's own suite, which both proves the real verdict replays to one hash AND that the harness CATCHES a deliberately non-deterministic command (the leak detector).

items.title: Contract enforced at spawn (PreToolUse gate)
items.intent: 'Subagents go through the contract' becomes a 100% gate, not a convention: a PreToolUse hook denies any Agent/Task spawn carrying NOVAKAI-CONTRACT:<id> when <id> has no valid+coherent contract, and fails open for every other spawn so recon/verify/analysis are never blocked.

items.title: Parallel execution scheduler (novakai:waves)
items.intent: Per-change contracts make ONE change executable; this turns them into a fan-out plan. novakai:waves computes the deterministic topological waves of a plan from dependsOn + live build status (routes to status.mjs): wave 0 = unbuilt changes whose deps are all built (dispatch to parallel subagents now), each later wave unlocking as the prior lands; built deps don't block, cycles are detected not looped. Output is byte-deterministic (ids sorted, content-hashed).

items.title: Subagent contract v2 — enforced edit-scope + SubagentStop verdict
items.intent: The subagent contract stops being voluntary. A subagent's Edit/Write is scoped by its spawn contract's editScope (scope.mjs matchScope over the packet's allow list + FROZEN deny globs: deny blocks, out-of-allow warns, no resolvable contract blocks repo writes); and when a contract subagent finishes, a SubagentStop hook routes to verify-change and writes a machine verdict to .novakai-verdicts/ (never blocking the stop). Closes the two holes: edit-gate previously covered only the main agent, and subagent completion was unhooked so the verdict rested on the agent's own report.

items.title: Behavioural contract for ctx/DOM-bound code (acceptance projection)
items.intent: Keystone-2 widened: acceptance cases can carry kind:'projection' — a JSON fixture (ctx-substitute) + a pure projection lens — so DOM/ctx-bound behaviour can be proven, not only pure exported functions. Closes the gap where 16/17 changes were shape-only (PASS_UNPROVEN). Determinism preserved (inert JSON + Function-constructed expressions, replayable).

items.title: Editor emits the human decision artifact
items.intent: On approve, the editor downloads approved-plan.json = {...plan, verdicts} (the human's accept/reject decisions as data), which the CLI approve-export.mjs consumes (--accepted-only) to mint the SAME contract bundle. The Plan type carries verdicts and normalizePlan preserves it. The AI->human->AI loop closes on a verifiable artifact, not an opaque .mmd.

items.title: verify-change --strict (no silent over-trust)
items.intent: PASS_UNPROVEN must not exit 0 like PASS. --strict makes the exit code require verdict==='PASS'; the JSON body is byte-identical in both modes. Removes the over-trust trap a downstream orchestrator would otherwise inherit from the exit code.

items.title: Orchestrator driver + per-change worktree isolation (novakai:orchestrate)
items.intent: The autonomous driver that runs the spine: route to waves.mjs for the dispatchable set, emit each change's contract, isolate it in a git worktree, collect its (strict-aware) verify-change verdict, and emit a canonical content-hashed summary. Byte-deterministic stdout (no time/random/paths), proven by replay. Turns the per-task contract into an actually-run fan-out.

items.title: Handoff content-falsifiability (claims, not just mtime)
items.intent: handoff:check is extended past the mtime comparison to fail on a machine-falsifiable content claim: a 'Not yet committed' assertion about files git shows as committed exits 1. A handoff can no longer pass its own freshness gate while asserting a falsehood.

items.title: App regression net
items.intent: Protect src/ (the app, 53 modules) from silent breakage: PR-gated typecheck, the orphaned characterization suite wired into CI, Playwright journeys + golden screenshots for the DOM/SVG surfaces node:test can't reach, and an append-only behavioural acceptance corpus harvested from completed plans so a proof made last month still guards this month.

items.title: Tooling self-map
items.intent: The dev-time .mjs tooling that runs the whole loop is itself represented in the ONE repo map (docs/novakai/_bundle.mmd — tooling fragments under tools/**/*.novakai.mmd bundle in alongside src/; the ts-morph checkers skip non-src anchors). It is architectural not a file-mirror (novakai-lint), complete (every load-bearing tools/ module is a node or an audited allowlist entry), symbol-true (every %% src resolves to a real definition) and deterministic (byte-identical re-bundle) — all proven by novakai:tooling:verify. Closes the gap where the map covered src/ only and the tooling that maintains it was invisible.
